business.headline: Oil shock deepens as West Asia war escalates. Is a global recession coming?The world is already feeling the heat as the West Asia conflict enters its fourth week, pushing global crude oil prices higher. In this article, we speak to experts to understand whether this shock could tip the world toward a recession.

Govt nod to hike commercial LPG allocation to 50% as domestic output improvesThe additional allocation will be subject to commercial establishments registering with oil companies and applying for a piped natural gas connection, according to a letter written by the oil secretary to states.

Flight tickets likely to get costlier from April 1. Here's whyCivil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu said that the impact of the ongoing Iran war on the aviation sector is likely to be visible from April 1, when the Air Turbine Fuel (ATF) prices will be revised.

Raymond's Chairman Gautam Singhania injured as his speedboat overturns in MaldivesGautam Singhania sustained minor injuries after a speedboat capsized in the Maldives. Two Indian nationals remain missing following the accident.

May lead to spike in fares: Airlines oppose 60% free seat ruleA new government directive on free seat allocation has triggered sharp opposition from airlines, who warn it could push up fares, hurt affordability and reshape how tickets are priced across India's aviation sector.

Why bank lockers are hard to get and what you can do insteadThis is the third part of Lock & Key, a series that looks at how India's bank locker system works, the risks involved, and what customers need to know. After examining safety and insurance in the first two parts, this story focusses on a new problem – access.

World economy in serious trouble, India vulnerable: Raghuram Rajan on oil crisisThe former RBI Govenor said a 15–20% global energy disruption would require sharp demand cuts, pushing oil to $150–$200 per barrel, risking severe economic stress if the conflict continues another month.

Oil could hit $150-$200 per barrel if Iran war drags on: Raghuram RajanFormer RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan warned that if the US-Israeli war against Iran continues beyond a month, oil prices could surge to $150-$200 per barrel, potentially triggering a global recession.

Premium petrol prices up by Rs 2, no change in normal petrol, diesel ratesIndian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL) and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) have increased premium petrol prices by ₹2 per litre, with IOCL XP95 now priced at ₹101.80 per litre. The hike affects premium petrol used mostly in performance vehicles, while regular petrol prices remain unchanged.
Sensex ends 326 points higher, Nifty above 23,100; HDFC Bank falls 2%The S&P BSE Sensex added 325.72 points to end at 74,532.96, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 112.35 points to end at 23,114.50.

Zomato hikes food delivery platform fee to Rs 14.90 from Rs 12.50 per orderThe platform fee now stands at Rs 14.90 per order on a pre-GST basis, up from Rs 12.50 earlier. This marks another round of increase, with the last hike having taken place in September 2025.

Flipkart CFO Sriram Venkataraman to step down ahead of planned IPOVenkataraman joined Flipkart in 2015 and has been part of the company's leadership team through a period of growth and restructuring. During his tenure, he helped strengthen the company's financial operations.

Premium petrol prices hiked by Rs 2 per litre; regular fuel unchangedThe hike applies to high-octane fuels such as Indian Oil's XP95 and Hindustan Petroleum's Power petrol, based on dealer inputs from select cities.

Is the 20-minute neighbourhood the future of urban living?Urban planners across global cities increasingly speak of the "20-minute neighbourhood", a model where residents can access most aspects of daily life within roughly twenty minutes.

Buying insurance online? 8 in 10 users say it can lead to problemsA nationwide survey has found that more than 80% of users faced issues such as hidden charges, difficulty with cancelling policies, forced data sharing and misleading pricing while using online insurance platforms.

Gold prices today: Why are rates rising after a steep fall and should you buy?Gold prices are edging higher after a sharp fall, but the trend remains uncertain as global cues and a strong dollar continue to drive volatility.

HDFC Bank share price extends fall, down 2% after sharp sell-offThe stock slipped 2.19% to Rs 782.15 on the BSE at 10:35 am, adding to losses after it had plunged more than 5% in the previous session, marking one of its sharpest declines in recent months.

Rupee hits all-time low, slips past Rs 93 mark against US dollarThe rupee weakened to Rs 93.15 against the US dollar in early trade, extending recent losses amid rising geopolitical tensions and a surge in crude oil prices.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is stock market rising today? 3 key reasonsThe rebound comes as easing oil prices, positive global cues, strong buying in IT stocks, and broad-based buying after Thursday's sharp fall lifted investor sentiment.

Is Dubai's luxury housing boom starting to crack as Gulf conflict escalates?Dubai's property market faces a key test as tensions rise, sentiment weakens, and luxury villa deals come under pressure, with the outlook hinging on how demand and geopolitical risks evolve.

entertainment.headline: Deepika Padukone's silence on Ranveer's Dhurandhar 2 bugs trolls. Should we care? The internet is losing it over Deepika Padukone's silence on Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2. But since when did a happy marriage require an Instagram post? The trolling reflects the classic double standard: Demanding wives be public cheerleaders while ignoring their right to a private life.

This album marks a new chapter where each member's unique voice contributes to a fluid, dynamic collective sound.

Meet Pari Pandher: The breakout star behind Jasleen Rangi in Dhurandhar 2 The emotional sibling reunion scene between Jaskirat Rangi and Jasleen Rangi in Dhurandhar 2 has won hearts, marking a strong Bollywood debut for Punjabi actor Pari Pandher.

Singer Justin Timberlake's 2024 arrest bodycam footage released Justin Timberlake's 2024 DWI arrest footage from New York's Hamptons has been released despite his legal efforts to keep it private. The Grammy winner pleaded guilty to a lesser charge and completed community service.

Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar 2 politics explained: Anti-terror, not anti-Pakistan Aditya Dhar doesn't make it easy to watch Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He lets you experience the discomfort, the silence that haunts and the screams that linger longer than you want. But at the centre of it all is India's clear policy: anti-terror, not anti-Pakistan. Consider this your spoiler warning.

Justin Bieber, Usher in tense exchange at 98th Oscars after-party: Report The 98th Academy Awards after-party saw a reported clash between Justin Bieber and Usher. Their long-time mentor-protege bond now appears strained, with neither addressing the incident publicly.

Dhurandhar's wildest twist belongs to Jameel Jamali, and fans weren't ready for it Spoiler alert: Dhurandhar: The Revenge flips the script with a quiet but powerful reveal – Jameel Jamali as the true mastermind. The twist reshapes the story, turning it into a game of control, patience, and hidden power.

Shah Rukh Khan wishes 'love and light' on Eid, fans say 'we got our eidi' Shah Rukh Khan extended heartfelt Eid greetings to his fans, wishing them peace, joy and prosperity on this festive occasion. He is gearing up for his upcoming film King, set for release in December 2026.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 3: Rs 500 crore worldwide for Ranveer's juggernaut Dhurandhar: The Revenge has breached the Rs 500 crore mark worldwide in just three days. This festive box office surge was always expected from the Aditya Dhar directorial, and it is set to grow even bigger on Sunday. Check the detailed collection report here.

Rashmika Mandanna-Vijay Deverakonda's cosy Thailand honeymoon pic melts internet Newlyweds Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda are winning hearts with a romantic photo from their Thailand honeymoon. The couple got married in February 2026.

Shakira's India shows called off amid Iran war, fans frustrated Shakira's India concerts have been postponed due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, leaving fans disappointed. Organisers have assured a full refund and said new dates will be announced soon.

BTS 2.0 begins: OT7 returns to stage for their first-ever live after 4 years BTS made a powerful return with a free concert at Gwanghwamun Square, drawing over two lakh fans on ground and millions online. The performance marked a new chapter, showcasing their evolved sound and strong group identity.

The 50 grand finale: When and where to watch; finalists, prize money and details The 50 is set for its grand finale on March 22, with the top 12 contestants competing. The finale will stream on JioHotstar and air on Colors TV.

Salman Khan and father Salim Khan greet fans, send wishes on Eid. Watch video On Eid 2026, veteran screenwriter Salim Khan made a cheerful public appearance with his family, greeting and waving at fans. His presence on Saturday delighted many who stood outside his Mumbai home.

Ranveer Singh's intense underwater prep for Dhurandhar 2 stuns fans Ranveer Singh is earning praise for performing a difficult underwater scene without a body double in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. The film has also opened strongly at the box office, earning over Rs 240 crore worldwide.

BTS Arirang 2026 Highlights: BTS 2.0 just getting started, says J-Hope BTS made a high-stakes comeback with their Arirang 2026 show on Saturday. The ARMY witnessed the group's first live performance after their hiatus following military enlistment and solo projects. The concert streamed live on Netflix from Seoul.

Kangana Ranaut calls Aditya Dhar a superstar director after Dhurandhar 2 success Kangana Ranaut praised Aditya Dhar as a "superstar director" following the massive opening of Dhurandhar: The Revenge. She highlighted the recognition Indian filmmakers often lack.

RGV calls Dhurandhar 2 a reset button for India cinema in new review of film Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma hailed Dhurandhar: The Revenge as a "reset button for Indian cinema." He called it a benchmark for future films.

Rakesh Bedi shares Aditya Dhar's emotional speech from inside Dhurandhar 2 screening Aditya Dhar expressed gratitude as Dhurandhar 2 received overwhelming love. He thanked his team during the film's premiere in Mumbai.

general news.headline: All but enemy-linked vessels: Iran softens stance on allowing ships via HormuzThe statement comes hours after US President Donald Trump threatened to target Iranian power plants if the waterway does not "fully open" within 48 hours.

If US targets our nukes, we'll bomb Delhi, Mumbai: Ex-Pak envoy's bizarre remarkA controversial remark by a former Pakistani diplomat has triggered concern amid rising regional tensions, with references to India drawing sharp reactions as geopolitical fault lines appear to widen further.

Russian oil tanker bound for China arrives in India; first of seven vesselsOil tanker Aqua Titan carrying Russian crude arrived at the New Mangaluru port on Saturday evening. The re-routing of such vessels comes after Indian refiners stepped up purchases of Russian oil following the 30-day waiver.

Deepika Padukone's silence on Ranveer's Dhurandhar 2 bugs trolls. Should we care?The internet is losing it over Deepika Padukone's silence on Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2. But since when did a happy marriage require an Instagram post? The trolling reflects the classic double standard: Demanding wives be public cheerleaders while ignoring their right to a private life.

I was offered lavender marriage. A closer look revealed the dark realityI was proposed lavender marriage twice within five months. Such moments seemingly unfold daily on matrimonial apps as queer people turn to this arrangement for social camouflage. A woman is offered a life of opulence abroad, but the emotional cost is huge and legal guardrails near-zero.

Ambling off Air Force One, struggling to sit down: Trump sparks health concernsA brief moment caught on camera has reignited debate around Donald Trump's health, with another clip adding fuel to speculation as online reactions grow sharper and questions around his fitness return.

Finding Mojtaba Khamenei: Iran Supreme Leader's whereabouts puzzle US, IsraelThe continued absence of Mojtaba, according to an Axios report, has been a matter of discussion during President Donald Trump's intelligence briefings. The report also quoted an official as saying that the US national security team is still assessing who is in charge in Tehran.

The ace up Iran's sleeve? What is a dirty bomb and will Tehran use one?With uranium enriched to 60%, it is not possible to build a nuclear bomb, but a radiological dispersal device can still be made. Such devices can be used to disperse radiological dust and smoke over a wide area. If Iran can make it, it could make Israel very uncomfortable.
The death of 'lived-in homes'In the race to design the "perfect" home, are we losing what made homes feel like ours?

Live: 1 killed after projectile fired from Lebanon strikes car in IsraelUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict in the Middle East witnessed an escalation on Sunday (IST) after a missile struck the city of Dimona, which hosts Israel's main nuclear research facility. This came as Iran said it was targeting Israel's nuclear programme in retaliation for an earlier strike on its nuclear enrichment facility in Natanz. Arad, another city in southern Israel, also came under a missile strike. The two incidents resulted in injuries to more than 100 people, including children. Israel has denied responsibility for the strike on Natanz. Hours after the strikes on Dimona and Arad, the Israeli military said it was conducting a fresh airstrike on Tehran. In another development, US President Donald Trump threatened Iran with strikes on its power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened "within 48 hours." Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East conflict.

Anaya Bangar undergoes gender change surgery, thanks father for supportAnaya Bangar shared that she has successfully undergone gender-affirming surgery in Thailand. She credited her father Sanjay Bangar and family for their support through the emotional journey.

Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar 2 politics explained: Anti-terror, not anti-PakistanAditya Dhar doesn't make it easy to watch Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He lets you experience the discomfort, the silence that haunts and the screams that linger longer than you want. But at the centre of it all is India's clear policy: anti-terror, not anti-Pakistan. Consider this your spoiler warning.

Tents, cars as homes: How Beirut's displaced families live amid Israeli strikesRows of makeshift tents and parked cars have turned into shelters on the outskirts of Beirut, as thousands of displaced families struggle to cope after evacuation orders amid the ongoing conflict between Israel Defense Forces and Hezbollah.

Ground reports by Ashraf Wani from the area show families, including children, spending nights in the open, with limited protection from weather and scarce access to basic necessities.

More than one million people have already been displaced across Lebanon as the conflict intensifies.

While some have managed to find shelter with relatives in safer areas, others have moved into temporary shelters. Many, however, have been left with no option but to stay in open spaces on the city's fringes.

In one such location, small tents dot open fields, offering little respite to families forced out of their homes.

Vehicles parked along the roadside have become improvised homes.

"Any car you see behind is a small home where families are sleeping for the night," said Ashraf Wani in a ground report from Beirut.

With large parts of the city ordered to be vacated, the pressure on both space and resources has intensified.

The scale of displacement has placed significant strain on the Lebanese government, with the situation fast evolving into a humanitarian crisis.

Despite the challenges, aid efforts continue.

Volunteers and non government groups are arriving daily to provide essentials such as food, water and breakfast to those stranded without support.

"Despite all these hardships, there are people coming every day… to serve food and water," Wani said.

As the conflict shows no immediate signs of easing, displaced families remain uncertain about how long they will have to live in such conditions.

Nvidia CEO says engineers should be evaluated on basis of how many AI tokens they useNvidia CEO Jensen Huang says the rise of AI is changing software engineering so much that companies may soon judge engineers by how many AI tokens they use instead of just how much code they write. He believes access to AI compute will become a key productivity tool, and engineers who use more AI effectively will deliver better results.

Is a global recession coming?The world is already feeling the heat as the West Asia conflict enters its fourth week, pushing global crude oil prices higher. In this article, we speak to experts to understand whether this shock could tip the world toward a recession.

Bengaluru police respond after video of Lamborghini doing stunts goes viralThe clip, shared by a user on X, captures a Lamborghini repeatedly doing donuts at a public junction under a flyover in Bengaluru, even as other vehicles pass by.

A silent imbalance: Women still donate the organs and men get themHandcrafted ByOur Editors Four out of five organ donors in India are women, and four out of five people who received the organs were men. The imbalance remains a telling reflection of society itself.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Missile strikes Israel's Dimona, home to key nuclear research site; several hurtThe strike on the desert city in southern Israel came hours after the Iranian media said US-Israeli forces had struck Iran's Natanz nuclear enrichment complex on Saturday morning. Israel's military has denied striking the Iranian facility.

Open Hormuz or will obliterate power plants: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to IranIn a social media post, Trump said the United States would "hit and obliterate" Iranian power plants, beginning with the largest facility, if the waterway is not "fully open, without threat" within the specified timeframe.

The AI Hiring Hypocrisy: Why AI is okay at work, but 'cheating' in job interviewsCompanies want AI-driven productivity at work, but penalise it in interviews. That contradiction in hiring practices is now raising questions bigger questions about outdated evaluation systems.

Battlefield to kitchen: How wars taught us to cook without cookingHandcrafted ByOur Editors As fuel prices rise and global tensions ripple into everyday life, the kitchen once again feels the shadow of war. In Part 2 of our ongoing series, we trace how battlefield innovations have reshaped how we eat today. Cooking, it seems, is no longer bound to the moment of hunger.

Air India tightens cabin crew fitness rules, weight gain may lead to pay lossA new policy at Air India could directly impact cabin crew rosters and salaries, linking fitness levels to employment status, as the airline moves to enforce stricter health standards from May 1.

It's back to Iranian oil for India after US waiver, but Tehran throws a spannerRefiners in India are planning to resume buying Iranian oil after the US lifted sanctions. It came as energy prices shot up amid the Iran war. It comes on the back of India already snapping up Russian oil currently stranded at sea.

sports.headline: Lucknow Super Giants make big call to help accommodate Rishabh Pant in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant is expected to be LSG's new No.3 while Nicholas Pooran gets demoted to the middle-order despite being one of their standout players last season.

Pakistan fined for poor T20 WC 2026: Kirsten says he isn't shocked about news Gary Kirsten said that he was sad to hear the news that the Pakistan players were being fined for their performance in the T20 World Cup 2026. Kirtsen, however, admitted that he wasn't surprised to hear about the move from PCB.

Gavaskar stands his ground, calls for IPL-owned teams to stop paying Pak players Sunil Gavaskar has called on Indian-owned franchises to stop paying Pakistani players, while hitting back at accusations of hypocrisy following his controversial column on the matter.

Premier League title race on hold as Manchester City, Arsenal eye Carabao Cup win With the Premier League title race put on hold for the moment, Arsenal and Manchester City will meet in the final of the Carabao Cup 2025-26. Will Sanju Samson be CSK's wicketkeeper in IPL 2026? Ashwin's post suggests otherwise IPL 2026: Ashwin's post showing Sanju Samson practising high catches in the deep with no keeping gloves has sparked speculation over his role at with the Chennai Super Kings ahead of the new season.

Chelsea thrashed by Everton, Liverpool's top 4 hopes dented as Brighton win 2-1 Liverpool and Chelsea stumble in the Premier League top 4 race after their losses to Brighton and Everton respectively. The Reds lost 1-2 at the Amex stadium, while Chelsea were thrashed 3-0 by Everton.

Wimbledon steps into new era, to introduce video review technology from 2026Wimbledon confirmed the introduction of video review technology from the 2026 edition. Players will be able to challenge umpire decisions without any limit on reviews.
